name: Keter Betts
abstract: William Thomas "Keter" Betts (July 22, 1928 – August 6, 2005) was an American jazz double bassist.

interviews.title: Keter Betts interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 11, 1996

concerts.label: Stan Getz and his Quartet with Special Guest Star João Gilberto

about_quotes.speaker: James Chirillo
about_quotes.evidence: I got to play with Tommy Flanagan and Keter Betts, Keter, who I played with plenty over the last few years until he just passed away like a year ago.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Chirillo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie Williams
about_quotes.evidence: I get a chance to be with my buddies Junior Mance and Keter Betts... We had such a great success the last time we were on the... we played the Song, the American Song Book.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie Williams interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Leggio
about_quotes.evidence: So that’s when I got Keter Betts, got June Bug, my buddy, and Louis Derry was on piano... And we went back and we had lines waiting to come in and see us.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Leggio interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Temperley
about_quotes.evidence: We have a new record coming out... that we recorded on the Norway, with Keter Betts on Bass and Jackie Williams on drums, Junior and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Temperley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part II
about_quotes.evidence: Keter Betts is the bass player... we were put together as a trio on the cruise... the three of us played
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part II interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 18, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Wendell Brunious
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve gotten to play with some of the greatest man. I mean that CD we did together... Keter Betts
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendell Brunious interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000 [interview 2 of 2]
